headline: Survey at 0.7% completion; early data shows strong partner satisfaction but concerning scale readiness gaps.
collection_status: Only 10 responses collected against 1,350 target (0.7% complete). Three regions active (MARA, SONGWE, KIGOMA) with seven regions untouched. Current pace projects 143 responses by survey close—a 89% shortfall requiring immediate acceleration.
key_findings: Volunteer respondents dominate (60%) over regional coordinators (20%) and assistant coordinators (20%), skewing sample toward ground-level perspective and away from leadership input.
Partner satisfaction exceptionally high at 4.8/5 contrasts sharply with scale readiness at 2.8/5, indicating stakeholders value current work but doubt organizational capacity for expansion.
Stakeholder priorities cluster around new program development (60%) over KiuFunza expansion (30%), suggesting appetite for diversification rather than deepening existing model.
Feedback themes emphasize teacher incentives, government integration, digital monitoring, and cross-stakeholder communication—operational enablers critical before scaling.
flags: Geographic coverage severely limited to 3/10 regions; risk of regional blind spots and unrepresentative findings undermining strategic decisions.
Scale readiness deficit (2.8/5) contradicts expansion priorities in feedback; organizational constraints (staffing, systems, coordination) may not support stated ambitions.
Response rate trajectory unsustainable; current collection velocity will miss target by ~1,200 responses without urgent intervention in recruitment and outreach.
recommendation: Immediately audit response collection channels and bottlenecks across inactive regions; simultaneously conduct rapid pulse interviews with 5-10 regional coordinators and education officials to validate scale readiness concerns before finalizing expansion strategy. This 1-2 week sprint will identify capability gaps blocking growth.
